summary: Premise-verified Session 83 audit applying the game/product lens and the founder-requested infrastructure rubric. Four locally executable findings survive live-code review: progression fan-out pre-claims a game before asynchronous legs finish while match history lacks durable replay protection; achievement reads expose arbitrary persistent-ID progress without the actor binding already used by season progression; the source-of-truth state ledger falsely reports the PostgreSQL-capable PlaytestEvidenceStore as always volatile; and the 2,900-line VaultFrontExecution embeds its breach-pressure state machine without a bounded composition contract. External staging, project-domain delivery, native Obelisk relying-party registration, three-human Alpha, revenue, rollback observation, and founder approval remain unobserved and are not audit implementation items.

sample.method: live code and contract survey; sample-codebase helper absent; manual survey capped to 20 primary files

nextMoves: Make the certified progression spine safely replayable after partial failure.
Protect private progression reads with the same actor contract as Season Pass.
Extract and exhaustively test the breach-pressure state machine without changing balance.
gameLoop.boundary: Public playlists, certified seasonal progression, prediction resolution, balance evidence, and visible entitlements are live in source. The remaining product uncertainty is empirical: no distinct-human Alpha corpus exists, so this audit does not invent tuning or retention claims.

passing: main-branch CI, E2E, semantic-release verification, and database workflow are green at 70781454
CANON-029 release and cost-bleeder gates pass as cost-neutral
sitemap, footer, legal/IP exception, local theme proof, and immutable promotion contracts are checked in
Brevo, Cloudflare, and Obelisk capabilities resolve READY through the secrets gateway
red: only the github-pages environment exists; no approved staging deployment/promotion receipt exists
no live origin health, exact-digest parity, headers, Core Web Vitals, or theme observation exists
no project-domain delivery or native Obelisk relying-party callback receipt exists
three-human Alpha, revenue, rollback-drill, and founder-approval receipts are absent

topRisks: partial progression failure is suppressed by a pre-claimed process-local game ID
player match history has no per-player/game replay uniqueness contract
achievement progress reads bypass the actor-bound trust model
readiness derives a false volatility warning from a stale handwritten state-scope row
VaultFrontExecution owns a large embedded pressure/breach state machine without a ratchet

evidenceBoundary: Local automation cannot manufacture staging, project-domain delivery, relying-party registration, distinct-human Alpha, revenue, rollback observation, or founder approval.
The registry still classifies VaultFront as app while local project truth says game. CANON-018 forbids editing the sibling registry; correction must travel through signed Ark cargo.
No dependency install, paid service, license change, sibling tree edit, or fabricated data is required for the four admitted items.
The latest visible CI failure belongs to the protobufjs 8.7.1 Dependabot pull request; main at 70781454 is green and remains pinned to trusted 7.6.5.

rejectedPremises.evidence: PlaytestEvidenceStore already writes and summarizes authenticated events in PostgreSQL. The valid bug is the stale StateScopeLedger declaration, not missing persistence.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Retune convoy rewards or breach timing from code review
rejectedPremises.evidence: The balance envelope is executable and deterministic, but no three-human Alpha corpus exists. Code review cannot honestly choose better human-facing values.
The main branch is intentionally pinned to trusted patched 7.6.5; the live Dependabot pull request fails the repository supply-chain contract because it crosses the approved major-version boundary.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Treat READY Cloudflare, Brevo, and Obelisk credentials as launch authorization
rejectedPremises.evidence: Credential readiness does not provide an approved staging origin, inbound route target, relying-party origin, or callback contract. Provider mutation without those targets would fabricate release progress.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Delete old workflows or TODO-marked upstream mechanics by age
rejectedPremises.evidence: The surveyed workflows and core mechanics remain referenced. Age and TODO text alone do not establish dead code or a VaultFront-specific defect.

rejectedPremises.candidate: Replace deterministic coaching with remote artificial intelligence
rejectedPremises.evidence: Certified deterministic prescriptions already preserve a cost-neutral baseline; no measured player outcome supports adding latency or variable inference to the core loop.

items.title: Turn certified progression into a replay-safe recoverable fan-out
items.why: MatchProgression inserts gameId into processedGameIds before its first asynchronous leg. A thrown leg leaves the ID claimed and every retry returns duplicate, while PlayerStatsStore can increment Elo and match counters again after restart because match_history has no (persistent_id, game_id) uniqueness. The visible progression stack is individually durable but its orchestration is not recoverable.
items.coreLoopStep: certified match result → rating/achievement/season fan-out → durable receipt
items.recipe: Make PlayerStatsStore atomically idempotent per game across memory and PostgreSQL, add the schema uniqueness invariant, replace pre-claim suppression with explicit in-flight/completed state that releases failed claims, and add concurrency, partial-failure, and fresh-spine replay tests proving each player/game changes rating once while failed fan-out can resume.

L2.recipe: Add store-level player/game idempotency and failure-retry tests.

L3.recipe: Add PostgreSQL advisory serialization plus unique schema protection, concurrent same-process promise coalescing, restart-safe downstream replay, and a canonical completion receipt digest.

executionLog: L3 shipped: PostgreSQL advisory serialization and a player/game unique index backstop restart replay; memory deduplicates the same contract; the progression spine coalesces concurrent calls, releases failed attempts for safe retry, and publishes a stable SHA-256 completion receipt. Focused replay, partial-failure, concurrency, and TypeScript gates passed.

items.title: Make achievement progression an actor-bound profile contract
items.why: GET /api/vaultfront/achievements/:persistentId and the meta-chain variant accept any caller-supplied persistent ID and expose progress without a play token. Newer season and contract reads authenticate the actor and reject cross-player claims. Achievement progress is private player progression and should share that boundary.
items.coreLoopStep: authenticated player → private progression profile → visible Command Center/debrief
items.recipe: Extract an AchievementRouter with injected authentication, bind optional path claims to the verified actor, update the client to send VaultFront identity headers, retain a compatibility path that fails closed on mismatch, add direct router tests, and include the router in the Worker composition budget.

L1.recipe: Require authentication on the two reads.

L2.recipe: Bind the path claim, update the client, and add authorization tests.

L3.recipe: Extract an injected router, test missing/cross-player/valid reads directly, and ratchet Worker composition so the trust boundary cannot drift back.

executionLog: L3 shipped: an injected AchievementRouter requires a verified play actor, rejects cross-player and malformed claims, rate-limits reads, updates client identity headers, and is protected by direct authorization tests plus the Worker composition ratchet (3,098/3,130 lines).

items.title: Make persistence readiness derive from executable store capabilities
items.why: StateScopeLedger declares playtest-pulse as process-only and release-critical even though Worker writes authenticated events through PlaytestEvidenceStore, which uses PostgreSQL when configured and labels process-local fallback honestly. Readiness can therefore emit a false volatility warning from stale handwritten metadata.
items.coreLoopStep: authenticated playtest evidence → durable pulse → honest Alpha/release readiness
items.recipe: Correct the playtest evidence owner/scope, represent store capability separately from effective runtime scope, self-validate declared database-capable owners against an executable capability catalog, and add tests proving ready/absent/failed database postures produce truthful pass/warn/block outcomes.

L1.recipe: Correct the stale playtest-pulse ledger row.

L2.recipe: Test the row against all database postures.

L3.recipe: Add an executable store-capability catalog and a ledger integrity assertion so owner/scope drift fails tests instead of lying in readiness.

executionLog: L3 shipped: PlaytestEvidenceStore is correctly modeled as PostgreSQL-capable with process-local development fallback; capability and effective scope are distinct; contradictory metadata blocks the ledger; disabled/ready/failed database tests and readiness/passport integration are green.

items.axis: feature depth / game loop / speed / testability
items.title: Extract the Vault Pressure climax into a deterministic domain kernel
items.why: VaultFrontExecution is roughly 2,900 lines and directly owns the three-delivery breach threshold, 90-second expiry, win trigger, and fallback-to-two behavior inside mutable maps. That flagship climax has no pure transition contract or file-size ratchet, making balance-preserving changes harder to prove than the newer reward planner.
items.coreLoopStep: convoy deliveries → pressure escalation → breach window → extraction win or recoverable expiry
items.recipe: Extract a pure VaultPressureKernel that receives prior state plus certified transition input and emits next state/events, preserve the current three-delivery and expiry semantics exactly, adapt VaultFrontExecution as composition only, add exhaustive transition/property tests, and add a source composition ratchet that forbids the extracted state-machine body from returning.

L1.recipe: Extract pressure arithmetic into pure helpers.

L2.recipe: Model delivery/expiry transitions and cover threshold edge cases.

L3.recipe: Emit typed domain events, exhaustively verify deterministic transition sequences, integrate without balance changes, and ratchet the composition boundary.

executionLog: L3 shipped: the three-delivery breach climax is a pure typed transition kernel with open, expiry, final-tick, normalization, victory, and deterministic sequence coverage. VaultFrontExecution composes the kernel without balance changes and is ratcheted at the exact formatter-stable 2,917-line baseline.
